essence: Performs laboratory testing on environmental samples to monitor pollution, protect ecosystems, and support regulatory compliance.

identity.title: Environmental Laboratory Technician
identity.summary: Analyses water, soil, and air samples in a laboratory setting, using scientific instruments to detect contaminants and support environmental protection.

salary.notes: Technicians in advanced labs or specialised analytical roles can exceed £48k-£60k+.

qualifications: Training in laboratory science or environmental science
Knowledge of sample preparation and analytical techniques
Ability to operate laboratory instruments (e.g., spectrometers, chromatographs)
Understanding of contamination control and quality assurance

lifestyle.description: Environmental laboratory technicians work in scientific labs for environmental agencies, water companies, research organisations, and private testing facilities. They analyse samples, maintain equipment, and ensure accurate reporting. The role suits individuals who enjoy hands‑on science, precision, and contributing to environmental protection.
